channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Israeli strike in northern Lebanon kills at least 18 people far from Hezbollah's main strongholds
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — An Israeli airstrike hit an apartment building in northern Lebanon on Monday, killing at least 18 people, according to the Lebanese Red Cross. There was no immediate comment from the Israeli military and it was not clear what the target was.
item.dc:creator: KAREEM CHEHAYEB, WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Vial that contained nerve agent that killed UK woman contained enough poison to kill thousands
item.description: LONDON (AP) — The lead counsel for a public inquiry into the 2018 death of a British woman poisoned by a Soviet-developed nerve agent said Monday that there was enough poison in the vial she unwittingly opened to kill thousands of people.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Middle East latest: EU condemns attacks on peacekeepers in Lebanon
item.description: The European Union on Monday condemned attacks on U.N. peacekeepers in Lebanon and rejected Israeli allegations that the U.N. was keeping them there to obstruct military operations against Hezbollah.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Former officer with East Germany’s secret police sentenced to prison for a border killing in 1974
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — An 80-year-old former officer with communist East Germany’s secret police, the Stasi, was sentenced Monday to 10 years in prison for the murder of a Polish man at a border crossing in divided Berlin 50 years ago.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: In Denmark, 50 well-preserved Viking Age skeletons have been unearthed, a rare discovery
item.description: Archaeologists in Denmark have unearthed a Viking Age burial ground containing some 50 “exceptionally well-preserved” skeletons.
item.dc:creator: James Brooks, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Russia prosecutors seek 3 1/4-year jail term for French citizen accused of collecting military data
item.description: Prosecutors have asked a Russian court to deliver a prison sentence of three years and three months to a French citizen arrested on charges of unlawfully collecting information on military issues.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Russia says it captured a southern Ukraine village in a push before winter comes
item.description: Russia says it has captured the village of Levadne in southern Ukraine as it probes for weaknesses along the war’s roughly 1,000-kilometer or 600-mile front line before winter arrives.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: UN peacekeepers in southern Lebanon are in the crosshairs of Israel’s war on Hezbollah
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — As the war between Israel and Lebanon's Hezbollah group escalates, a United Nations peacekeeping force in Lebanon is increasingly in the crosshairs, with Israeli troops firing at the peacekeepers' headquarters and positions several times in the past week.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Netanyahu mulls plan to empty northern Gaza of civilians and cut off aid to those left inside
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu is examining a plan to seal off humanitarian aid to northern Gaza in an attempt to starve out Hamas militants, a plan that, if implemented, could trap without food or water hundreds of thousands of Palestinians unwilling or unable to leave t
item.dc:creator: JULIA FRANKEL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden-Harris admin accused of ‘too little, too late’ to save the people of war-torn, famine-stricken Sudan
item.description: Critics say the Biden administration is in danger of waiting too long to stop the killing and help the people of Sudan get humanitarian aid, as it makes a last-minute effort to make a difference.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Middle East latest: US says it will send THAAD defense battery to Israel, along with US troops
item.description: The U.S. is sending a Terminal High Altitude Area Defense battery to Israel, along with US troops to operate it. The Pentagon made the announcement just hours after Iran warned the U.S. to keep its military forces out of Israel.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: China deploys record 125 warplanes in large scale military drill in warning to Taiwan
item.description: Taiwan's defense ministry says China used 125 military aircraft in exercises aimed at Taiwan, a record for a single day.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Iran making billions in oil revenues under Biden admin as expert slams sanctions policy for lack of 'pressure'
item.description: Some critics have claimed the revenues do not paint an accurate picture due to the fluctuation of prices and lack of reliable sources, but an expert argued that other reports confirm an export surge.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Columbus remains, verified after 500 years, show he was Jewish: documentary
item.description: A 22-year investigation by Spanish forensic researchers concluded that Christopher Columbus was a Sephardic Jew from Western Europe.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Former Scottish leader Alex Salmond, who nearly got Scotland's independence from UK, dies at 69
item.description: Alex Salmond, the former first minister of Scotland and a prominent advocate for Scottish independence, has died at the age of 69 in Ohrid, North Macedonia.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Indian politician known for his close ties with Bollywood is killed in Mumbai
item.description: NEW DELHI (AP) — A senior politician in India’s financial capital, Mumbai, who was also known for his close ties with Bollywood has been shot dead weeks before a key state election.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Middle East latest: Palestinian officials say 22 killed in Israeli airstrikes
item.description: The Israeli military on Saturday renewed its orders for Palestinians in the northern Gaza Strip to leave their homes and shelters as troops continue a weeklong offensive against militants.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A-bomb survivors use Nobel Peace Prize to share anti-nuke message with the young
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — The recipient of this year's Nobel Peace Prize is a fast-dwindling group of atomic bomb survivors who are facing down the shrinking time they have left to convey the firsthand horror they witnessed 79 years ago. Nihon Hidankyo, the Japanese organization of survivors of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Boris Johnson says Biden told him his family origins 'were not really Irish at all'
item.description: Boris Johnson, former United Kingdom prime minister, said President Biden told him he was not Irish, according to his new memoir "Unleashed," which was published Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Mount Everest remains believed to be climber who vanished 100 years ago
item.description: A National Geographic documentary crew has found the possible partial remains of Andrew Irvine, a British climber who vanished on Mount Everest 100 years ago.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russia launches 4th aerial attack in a week against Ukraine's grain-exporting Odesa region
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — A nighttime Russian missile strike on Odesa killed at least four people including a 16-year-old girl, regional authorities said Friday, in the latest in a series of attacks this week on the southern Ukrainian region that are likely intended to disrupt the country’s grain exports
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Iran's national airliner accused of flying weapons to Beirut airport for terror proxy Hezbollah: Report
item.description: Iran’s domestic airline, already sanctioned for support to Russia, is being accused of aiding Hezbollah by an Iranian dissident group who has called for additional sanctions to be put on the carrier.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Gender of female captain not to blame for sinking of $61M navy ship: New Zealand defense minister
item.description: New Zealand's Defense Minister has rebukes claims that the appointment of a female to captain a $61 million navy ship ultimately led to its sinking.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: At least 22 killed in airstrikes in central Beirut, with Israel also firing on UN peacekeepers
item.description: The air raid on central Beirut — the deadliest in over a year of war — apparently targeted two residential buildings in separate neighborhoods.
item.dc:creator: BILAL HUSSEIN, WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: British YouTuber and rapper Yung Filly is charged with raping and choking a woman in Australia
item.description: PERTH, Australia (AP) — A British YouTuber and rapper known as Yung Filly has been charged with raping and choking a woman in a hotel room following an Australian music performance.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Yemen’s Houthi rebels attack a Liberian-flagged ship in the Red Sea
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Yemen's Houthi rebels attacked a Liberian-flagged chemical tanker on Thursday in the Red Sea, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Brown votes against student proposal to divest from companies allegedly connected to war in Gaza
item.description: Brown University announced Thursday it will not divest from companies that pro-Palestinian students say contribute to Israel's war efforts in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, The Hill

item.title: Israeli defense minister warns an attack on Iran would be 'lethal' and 'surprising'
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israel's defense minister warned on Wednesday that his country's retaliation for a recent Iranian missile attack will be “lethal” and “surprising,” while the Israeli military pushed ahead with a large-scale operation in northern Gaza and a ground offensive in Lebanon against Hezboll
item.dc:creator: TIA GOLDENBERG, SAMY MAGDY and WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Google loses final EU court appeal against 2.4 billion euro fine in antitrust shopping case
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Google lost its final legal challenge on Tuesday against a European Union penalty for giving its own shopping recommendations an illegal advantage over rivals in search results, ending a long-running antitrust case that came with a whopping fine.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Former hostage wants ‘peace in the world’ one year later
item.description: One year later, the Chicago-area woman is steadfast in her faith and hopeful others captured that day will be released.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation
